summary: Marketing surfaces for API, infrastructure, and metered SaaS where pricing is a function of consumption. The page must legibly answer 'what does this cost at my volume?' before the bottom of the page.

patterns.name: API snippet with meter aside
patterns.summary: Hero with copy on the left and a paired code snippet plus a small visualization of the metered unit (requests, tokens, GB) on the right.

variants.summary: Snippet on top of the meter visualization.
Snippet and meter side by side in the right column.

patterns.name: Three-pillar technical brief
patterns.summary: Three feature cards anchored on technical primitives (e.g. 'Latency', 'Reliability', 'Observability'). Each card has a small chart or sparkline as its media element.

variants.summary: Each card includes an inline sparkline.
Each card uses an icon instead of a sparkline.

patterns.name: Architecture diagram block
patterns.summary: A labeled diagram showing how the product fits into the buyer's stack. 4–8 nodes, no more. Labels are short noun phrases.

variants.summary: Linear left-to-right with a single arrow flow.
Vertical layered stack from data plane to application.

anti_examples: Marketing-grade illustrations without labels are excluded.

patterns.name: Usage calculator
patterns.summary: An interactive widget where the visitor selects a volume (slider or input) and the page computes a transparent monthly estimate based on a published rate card.

variants.summary: One input maps to one meter (e.g. requests/month).
Multiple inputs (compute, storage, egress) compose into a total.

anti_examples: A pricing page with only static tiers is pricing/contact-tier-table; this pattern requires interactive volume.

patterns.name: Transparent rate card
patterns.summary: A small table listing each metered unit and its per-unit price. Adjacent to the calculator. Footnotes name volume discounts and committed-use plans.

variants.summary: One row per resource, one rate.

patterns.name: Availability and scale strip
patterns.summary: A row of 3–4 numbers describing platform-level scale (regions, uptime, average request latency) with explicit units.

variants.summary: Each metric has a 'see status' affordance to a public status page.

patterns.name: Billing-focused FAQ
patterns.summary: An accordion of 5–8 questions all narrowly focused on pricing edge cases — overage, free tier, refunds, currency, taxes.

variants.summary: Two-column static Q/A grid, no expand-collapse.

anti_examples: General product FAQs belong in faq/product-faq.

patterns.name: Sandbox CTA
patterns.summary: Conversion band offering a free sandbox or playground without sign-up. Primary CTA opens the sandbox; secondary links to docs.

variants.summary: Left-aligned heading, CTAs on the right.
